CAT01-060.headline: Emotions Across the Lifespan
CAT01-060.subtitle: How feelings change from childhood to old age
CAT01-060.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans slightly forward with one open palm extended, posture curious and unhurried, conveying a steady passage through the changing seasons of a life.
CAT01-060.sceneLine: A long row of soft blue-indigo rounded forms recedes gently beside the figure, each subtly different in size, suggesting one continuous emotional arc from small beginnings to quiet maturity.

CAT01-066.headline: Major Life Transitions
CAT01-066.subtitle: How big changes reshape the emotional landscape
CAT01-066.figureLine: A single standing clay figure pauses at the edge of an unseen threshold, one foot lifted to step, posture poised between hesitation and resolve.
CAT01-066.sceneLine: A wide blue-indigo accent landform gently shifts and re-forms into new contours around the figure's feet, the soft light blue-indigo terrain rearranging itself into fresh ground to walk on.

CAT02-046.headline: Parental Burnout
CAT02-046.subtitle: When raising children depletes everything
CAT02-046.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests with shoulders rounded and hands loose in its lap, posture emptied and weary, conveying a depletion that asks gently for rest.
CAT02-046.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo vessel beside the figure tips low and nearly drained, while a faint pale blue-indigo trace of refill begins to pool at its base, suggesting capacity that can slowly return.

CAT03-076.headline: Rebuilding Identity
CAT03-076.subtitle: After a long-term relationship ends
CAT03-076.figureLine: A single standing clay figure gathers itself upright again, one hand pressed to its own chest, posture tentative but newly self-contained.
CAT03-076.sceneLine: Scattered soft light blue-indigo fragments draw slowly back together beside the figure into one whole pale blue-indigo form, suggesting a self being patiently reassembled.

CAT05-051.headline: Career Change
CAT05-051.subtitle: The identity crisis of losing a job title
CAT05-051.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands with arms slightly open and empty, posture unmoored yet steady, conveying a self searching for new footing.
CAT05-051.sceneLine: A blue-indigo accent badge-like form dissolves softly from the figure's chest while a faint pale blue-indigo outline of a new shape begins to take form, suggesting identity remade beyond a title.

CAT05-056.headline: Retirement Anxiety
CAT05-056.subtitle: Preparing mental health for life after work
CAT05-056.figureLine: A single standing clay figure looks toward open space ahead with hands folded calmly at its waist, posture uncertain but composed.
CAT05-056.sceneLine: A structured blue-indigo accent lattice gently loosens into an open, soft light blue-indigo expanse beside the figure, suggesting a tightly held routine giving way to unscripted time.

CAT06-024.headline: Single Parenting
CAT06-024.subtitle: Stress, resilience, and support for one parent
CAT06-024.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds itself steady and upright while one protective arm curves around a small empty space at its side, posture resolute and capable.
CAT06-024.sceneLine: A single firm blue-indigo accent pillar rises beside the figure, its base widened by soft light blue-indigo supports, suggesting one person carrying weight while quietly held up from below.

CAT06-027.headline: Intensive Parenting Pressure
CAT06-027.subtitle: Why modern parenting feels exhausting
CAT06-027.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans under a felt weight on its shoulders, posture stretched thin yet still standing, conveying relentless expectation.
CAT06-027.sceneLine: A dense stack of soft light blue-indigo blocks looms above the figure, while a few pale blue-indigo pieces lift gently away, suggesting impossible demands that can begin to be set down.

CAT06-029.headline: Stay-at-Home Parenting
CAT06-029.subtitle: Identity, isolation, and invisible labor
CAT06-029.figureLine: A single seated clay figure works quietly with both hands at low, repetitive tasks, posture absorbed and unseen, conveying steady labor that goes unwitnessed.
CAT06-029.sceneLine: A faint pale blue-indigo glow gathers softly around the figure's working hands, suggesting unseen effort gently made visible and honored.

CAT06-061.headline: Caught in the Middle
CAT06-061.subtitle: Caring for children and aging parents at once
CAT06-061.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands centered with both arms gently extended to either side, posture balanced and giving, conveying care pulled in two directions.
CAT06-061.sceneLine: Two soft light blue-indigo forms rest on either side of the figure, one smaller and one larger, held in steady blue-indigo accent balance, suggesting care held in both hands at once.

CAT06-062.headline: Caregiver Burnout
CAT06-062.subtitle: When caring for an aging parent depletes you
CAT06-062.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits with head bowed low and hands resting open and tired, posture spent from long giving.
CAT06-062.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo lantern beside the figure glows faintly low, while a quiet pale blue-indigo current drifts back toward it, suggesting depleted warmth slowly being restored.

CAT06-063.headline: Hard Conversations
CAT06-063.subtitle: Talking with aging parents about driving and finances
CAT06-063.figureLine: Two clay figures sit turned toward each other at a respectful, gentle distance, postures attentive and careful, conveying a tender, difficult exchange held with care.
CAT06-063.sceneLine: A slender blue-indigo accent thread passes softly between the two figures across the space between them, suggesting a delicate conversation carried with steadiness and respect.

CAT06-064.headline: Grieving a Declining Parent
CAT06-064.subtitle: Anticipatory grief as a parent fades
CAT06-064.figureLine: A single standing clay figure turns gently toward an empty space beside it, one hand reaching softly into the quiet, posture tender and bracing.
CAT06-064.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo form beside the figure fades very gradually toward pale blue-indigo at its edges, held within a protective blue-indigo glow, suggesting a presence cherished while it slowly grows faint.

CAT06-065.headline: Caring for Someone With Dementia
CAT06-065.subtitle: The mental health impact on family
CAT06-065.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands close and steady beside an empty space, one hand held gently open in patient reassurance, posture grounded and devoted.
CAT06-065.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo pattern beside the figure drifts and rearranges quietly, while a steady pale blue-indigo thread stays whole through it, suggesting constancy of care amid shifting memory.

CAT06-067.headline: Coping With Caregiver Guilt
CAT06-067.subtitle: After placing a loved one in a care facility
CAT06-067.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits with both hands pressed softly together near its chest, posture heavy with self-doubt yet seeking peace.
CAT06-067.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo weight rests on the figure's shoulders while a warm pale blue-indigo glow rises gently to meet it, suggesting guilt slowly easing into self-compassion.

CAT06-068.headline: The Financial Stress of Eldercare
CAT06-068.subtitle: And its impact on mental health
CAT06-068.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands beneath a felt pressure, both hands clasped tightly at its waist, posture braced and burdened.
CAT06-068.sceneLine: A heavy soft light blue-indigo mass hovers above the figure while slender pale blue-indigo supports rise beneath it, suggesting a weight made bearable when shared and supported.

CAT06-070.headline: Self-Care for Caregivers
CAT06-070.subtitle: Preventing burnout while caring for others
CAT06-070.figureLine: A single seated clay figure pauses to rest one hand gently over its own heart, posture finally turning care inward.
CAT06-070.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo vessel beside the figure is refilled slowly by a quiet pale blue-indigo stream, suggesting the caregiver pausing to replenish their own reserves.

CAT07-023.headline: Dual Process Model of Grief
CAT07-023.subtitle: Moving between loss and restoration
CAT07-023.figureLine: A single standing clay figure shifts its weight gently between two footings, posture swaying softly back and forth, conveying a natural movement through mourning.
CAT07-023.sceneLine: Two soft light blue-indigo tides rise and recede in gentle alternation on either side of the figure, suggesting an honest oscillation between sorrow and renewal.

CAT07-024.headline: Prolonged Grief Disorder
CAT07-024.subtitle: When grief does not ease over time
CAT07-024.figureLine: A single seated clay figure stays low and still, arms wrapped softly around itself, posture held long in sorrow yet quietly attended.
CAT07-024.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo weight settles close around the figure while a faint pale blue-indigo thread of light waits steadily at its edge, suggesting grief that lingers and care that stays near.

CAT07-025.headline: Anticipatory Grief
CAT07-025.subtitle: Mourning a loss before it arrives
CAT07-025.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands quietly with one hand resting over its chest, gaze turned toward what has not yet gone, posture tender and waiting.
CAT07-025.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo form rests near the figure beneath a protective blue-indigo glow, its edges only beginning to soften toward pale blue-indigo, suggesting love held close while loss approaches.

CAT07-026.headline: Disenfranchised Grief
CAT07-026.subtitle: When losses go unacknowledged
CAT07-026.figureLine: A single seated clay figure holds a small sorrow cupped in its hands, set slightly apart from the world, posture quiet and unseen.
CAT07-026.sceneLine: A small pale blue-indigo light glows in the figure's cupped hands while the wider space stays dim, suggesting a real grief that deserves to be witnessed and named.

CAT08-026.headline: Adapting Routines
CAT08-026.subtitle: When life becomes chaotic
CAT08-026.figureLine: A single standing clay figure steadies itself amid motion, one foot planted firmly while the other adjusts, posture finding balance in disorder.
CAT08-026.sceneLine: Scattered soft light blue-indigo forms swirl loosely around the figure, while a few settle into a calm pale blue-indigo line at its feet, suggesting order gently re-emerging from chaos.

CAT12-038.headline: Reverse Culture Shock
CAT12-038.subtitle: The hidden struggle of coming home
CAT12-038.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands in a once-familiar place, posture slightly turned and unsettled, conveying a homecoming that no longer feels like home.
CAT12-038.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo doorway frame stands before the figure, its interior shimmering faintly different in pale blue-indigo, suggesting a return to a place quietly changed.

CAT14-045.headline: Recovering From Caregiver Fatigue
CAT14-045.subtitle: Recognizing and replenishing your reserves
CAT14-045.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans back to rest at last, hands unclenching and shoulders loosening, posture softening into recovery.
CAT14-045.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo glow gathers slowly back around the resting figure, brightening gently toward pale blue-indigo, suggesting depleted energy quietly returning.

CAT15-012.headline: Why College Can Feel Lonely
CAT15-012.subtitle: Profound isolation amid a crowd
CAT15-012.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands apart in open space, arms held close, posture quietly separate within a crowd it cannot quite reach.
CAT15-012.sceneLine: Several soft light blue-indigo forms cluster at a distance from the figure while one faint pale blue-indigo thread reaches tentatively across the gap, suggesting connection still within reach.

CAT15-014.headline: Loneliness in New Parents
CAT15-014.subtitle: The isolation nobody talks about
CAT15-014.figureLine: A single seated clay figure cradles a small empty space against its chest, posture tender yet quietly isolated in the long hours.
CAT15-014.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo glow surrounds the figure in a close, quiet circle while a faint pale blue-indigo path opens outward, suggesting isolation that can gently open to others.

CAT15-016.headline: Loneliness in Older Adults
CAT15-016.subtitle: A health crisis hidden in plain sight
CAT15-016.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests alone in a wide, still space, hands folded patiently, posture quiet and waiting to be seen.
CAT15-016.sceneLine: A single soft light blue-indigo light glows beside the seated figure within a vast dim expanse, while a pale blue-indigo thread reaches toward it, suggesting a quiet loneliness ready to be answered.

CAT15-017.headline: Loneliness After Retirement
CAT15-017.subtitle: Losing identity and social connection
CAT15-017.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands in newly emptied space with arms loose at its sides, posture adrift after a long-held role has fallen quiet.
CAT15-017.sceneLine: A blue-indigo accent web of connections thins around the figure while a few soft light blue-indigo strands hold firm, suggesting bonds that can be rebuilt after work recedes.

CAT15-018.headline: Loneliness After Loss
CAT15-018.subtitle: When grief removes your person
CAT15-018.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests beside an empty space once filled, one hand resting gently where another presence used to be, posture tender and bereft.
CAT15-018.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo glow lingers warmly in the empty space beside the figure beneath a protective pale blue-indigo light, suggesting a cherished presence remembered with care.

CAT17-001.headline: Mental Health in Later Life
CAT17-001.subtitle: What changes and what stays stable
CAT17-001.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands grounded and composed, one hand resting calmly at its side, posture settled into a steady later chapter.
CAT17-001.sceneLine: Some soft light blue-indigo forms shift and renew around the figure while a firm pale blue-indigo core holds constant at its center, suggesting change and steadiness held together.

CAT17-002.headline: Normal Aging vs. Mental Illness
CAT17-002.subtitle: How to tell the difference
CAT17-002.figureLine: A single standing clay figure looks attentively at two gentle forms before it, posture thoughtful and discerning.
CAT17-002.sceneLine: Two soft light blue-indigo forms rest side by side before the figure, one in smooth pale blue-indigo and one subtly clouded, suggesting the careful task of telling ordinary change from real concern.

CAT17-005.headline: Isolation in Older Adults
CAT17-005.subtitle: Social isolation and loneliness in later life
CAT17-005.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits within a wide quiet space, hands resting in its lap, posture still and gently waiting for company.
CAT17-005.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo halo encircles the seated figure while distant pale blue-indigo lights begin to drift nearer, suggesting separation easing toward connection.

CAT17-009.headline: Bereavement in Later Life
CAT17-009.subtitle: Loss, mourning, and moving forward
CAT17-009.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands quietly with one hand over its heart, gaze lowered in mourning yet feet ready to move on, posture tender and resilient.
CAT17-009.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo memory glows warmly beside the figure while a gentle pale blue-indigo path opens ahead, suggesting loss carried with care into the days that follow.

CAT17-010.headline: Suicide Prevention in Older Adults

CAT17-010.figureLine: A single seated clay figure is held gently within a warm, encircling space, posture low yet surrounded and supported, conveying a person not left alone.
CAT17-010.sceneLine: A protective soft light blue-indigo circle gathers closely around the figure while a steady pale blue-indigo light reaches in from beyond it, suggesting reaching out and being safely held.

CAT17-020.headline: Family Caregiving

CAT17-020.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands bearing a felt weight on its shoulders, posture giving and tired yet still upright.
CAT17-020.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo load rests on the figure while widening pale blue-indigo supports rise beneath it, suggesting a heavy role made steadier through understanding and help.

CAT17-021.headline: Professional Support Services

CAT17-021.sceneLine: Several steady blue-indigo accent supports move in to brace beside the figure, their pale blue-indigo edges interlocking gently, suggesting professional help arriving to share the load.

CAT17-023.headline: Advance Directives

CAT17-023.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits calmly with hands folded in quiet resolve, posture thoughtful and at peace with a difficult choice.
CAT17-023.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo path ahead of the figure resolves gently into a clear, settled pale blue-indigo line, suggesting a difficult decision met with calm and clarity.

CAT17-024.sceneLine: A row of soft light blue-indigo dwellings of varied form rests before the figure, each lit to a different warmth of pale blue-indigo, suggesting a careful comparison of places of care.

CAT17-028.sceneLine: A larger and a smaller soft light blue-indigo form rest on either side of the figure in careful pale blue-indigo equilibrium, suggesting the steady balancing of care for old and young.

CAT17-029.headline: Grief After Caregiving
CAT17-029.subtitle: Transitioning from caregiver to bereaved

CAT17-029.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo glow that once gathered at the figure's hands now drifts gently outward, while a faint pale blue-indigo path opens ahead, suggesting an identity gently released and a way forward found.

CAT17-030.headline: Recognizing Elder Abuse
CAT17-030.subtitle: Warning signs and how to report

CAT17-034.headline: Senior Centers

CAT17-034.sceneLine: Soft light blue-indigo forms gather in a warm circle ahead of the figure, joined by gentle pale blue-indigo threads, suggesting a welcoming community drawing the figure in.

CAT17-035.headline: Volunteering in Retirement
CAT17-035.subtitle: A source of purpose and mental health
CAT17-035.figureLine: A single standing clay figure extends both hands outward in willing offering, posture renewed by being useful again.

CAT17-038.headline: Overcoming Barriers to Socializing
CAT17-038.subtitle: Reconnecting in older adulthood
CAT17-038.figureLine: A single standing clay figure steps gently through a narrow opening toward others, posture tentative but determined.
CAT17-038.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo wall before the figure parts into a clear pale blue-indigo gateway, suggesting obstacles to connection gradually giving way.

CAT17-039.headline: Making Friends After 65
CAT17-039.subtitle: Strategies for social connection
CAT17-039.figureLine: Two clay figures turn warmly toward each other with open postures, conveying a new bond beginning to form.

CAT17-040.headline: Outliving Friends and Spouse
CAT17-040.subtitle: Grief and isolation in later life

CAT17-041.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo open expanse and a warm pale blue-indigo gathering rest on either side of the figure in quiet balance, suggesting a choice between independence and community.

CAT17-044.headline: Lasting Impact on Senior Isolation

CAT17-045.headline: Rebuilding a Support Network

CAT17-046.headline: Cumulative Loss in Older Adults

CAT17-046.sceneLine: Several soft light blue-indigo memory-lights gather softly around the figure beneath a protective pale blue-indigo glow, suggesting many losses held with care over time.

CAT17-047.headline: Losing a Spouse

CAT17-047.figureLine: A single standing clay figure gathers itself upright beside an empty space once shared, one hand on its own chest, posture grieving yet beginning to stand on its own.
CAT17-047.sceneLine: A soft light blue-indigo glow lingers warmly in the empty space beside the figure while scattered pale blue-indigo fragments draw together into a new whole, suggesting a self rebuilt after profound loss.

CAT17-048.headline: Retirement and Mental Health
CAT17-048.subtitle: Identity and purpose after work
CAT17-048.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands in newly open space with hands resting calmly, posture settling into a self beyond a working role.
CAT17-048.sceneLine: A structured blue-indigo accent form gently dissolves into an open soft light blue-indigo field, where a fresh pale blue-indigo shape begins to grow, suggesting purpose remade after work.

CAT17-049.headline: Moving and Downsizing

CAT19-009.headline: Retirement and Identity in Men
CAT19-009.subtitle: Rebuilding a sense of self after work
